topic: Devstral Small 2 Local Setup Guide 2026: Run Mistral Coding Agent on Your Laptop

key_points: Step-by-step Ollama setup for Devstral 2 locally
Quickstart guide for Mistral Devstral locally
Unsloth GGUF quantization for lower VRAM setups
Performance benchmarks at different quantization levels
Mistral Vibe CLI complete setup guide
100% free and private local AI coding

stats.fact: Devstral Small 2 scores 68.0% on SWE-bench Verified, matching models up to 5x its size
Devstral Small 2 has 24B parameters with a 256K context window, released December 10, 2025 under Apache 2.0
Devstral 2 (123B) achieves 72.2% on SWE-bench Verified; Claude Sonnet 4.5 scores 77.2% for comparison
Devstral 2 is up to 7x more cost-efficient than Claude Sonnet at real-world coding tasks
API pricing: $0.10/$0.30 per million tokens (input/output) — same as Mistral Small 3.1
Q4 quantization (Q4_K_M) delivers 95%+ quality at 40% reduced memory usage, ideal for 24GB VRAM GPUs
Mac M1/M2/M3/M4 with 32GB unified memory can run Devstral Small 2 without discrete GPU
Devstral Small 2 outperforms Qwen 3 Coder Flash (30B) despite being a 24B model

angles: First genuinely laptop-friendly cloud-grade coding agent — 68% SWE-bench on a single GPU
Apache 2.0 license means zero API fees, full code privacy, and on-device inference
Mac M-series unified memory architecture makes this easier to run than on Windows/Linux with discrete GPU
Replaces GitHub Copilot and Claude entirely for offline-first developers
Three setup paths: Ollama (beginner), vLLM (production), llama.cpp (CPU/low VRAM) — pick your level
Mistral Vibe CLI offers 'vibe coding' in terminal — like Cursor but 100% local
OpenHands integration enables fully autonomous multi-file coding without cloud dependency
256K context window means the model can reason over entire mid-size codebases in one shot
